title: Epilasia ammophila

The classification of Epilasia ammophila is further detailed in the botanical hierarchy as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Genus: Epilasia, Species: E. ammophila. intro: Epilasia ammophila, also known as Samoan dodder, is a type of parasitic vine native to the Pacific Islands, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Epilasia ammophila has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Epilasia ammophila include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Samoan medicine, Epilasia ammophila is often made into a tea or tincture, and it is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions.
